name: San Juan del Puerto, Beas, Valverde del Camino, La Palma del Condado, Niebla – circular route
name_original: San Juan del Puerto, Beas, Valverde del Camino, La Palma del Condado, Niebla – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around San Juan del Puerto traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by the unique reddish hues of the Tinto River, its extensive marshes, and the surrounding countryside. The region offers a mix of flat riverine paths and gently rolling terrain, providing varied cycling experiences. Historical sites linked to the Age of Discovery, such as the Muelle de las Carabelas, are often integrated into the routes. This area in Huelva, Spain, combines natural beauty with cultural significance, making it suitable for road cyclists seeking scenic and engaging rides. Best road cycling routes around San Juan Del Puerto The most popular road cycling route is San Juan del Puerto, Beas, Valverde del Camino, La Palma del Condado, Niebla – circular route, a 55.9 miles (89.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several towns, offering a comprehensive tour of the local area. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Columbian Places and Mazagón Coast Loop, a moderate 46.8 miles (75.3 km) path. This route combines historical landmarks with scenic coastal views along the Atlantic. Local road cyclists also love the Puerta del Buey – Niebla Castle loop from San Juan del Puerto, a 36.8 miles (59.3 km) trail leading through the historic town of Niebla, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around San Juan Del Puerto is defined by riverine paths along the Tinto River, open countryside, and routes passing through historical towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance rides. The routes in San Juan Del Puerto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Juan Del Puerto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around San Juan del Puerto?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes around San Juan del Puerto, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 2000 times.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of road cycling routes in San Juan del Puerto?
A: The routes around San Juan del Puerto cater to various abilities. You'll find approximately 24 easy routes, 51 moderate routes, and 9 more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near San Juan del Puerto?
A: Road cycling routes near San Juan del Puerto feature a diverse landscape. You can expect a mix of flat riverine paths along the Tinto River, gently rolling countryside, and sections that traverse coastal areas. The unique reddish hues of the Tinto River and its extensive marshes provide a distinctive backdrop for many rides.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in the San Juan del Puerto area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are routes with more significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Buey – Niebla loop from San Juan del Puerto&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering over 114 km with an elevation gain of more than 900 meters.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from San Juan del Puerto?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in San Juan del Puerto. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991483382/san-juan-del-puerto-beas-valverde-del-camino-la-palma-del-condado-niebla-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan del Puerto, Beas, Valverde del Camino, La Palma del Condado, Niebla – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 89.9 km ride connecting several towns.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history, particularly related to the Age of Discovery. Routes often pass by significant sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3155444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Discovering Faith&lt;/a&gt; at Punta del Sebo and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muelle de las Carabelas&lt;/a&gt; in Palos de la Frontera, which features replicas of Columbus's ships. You might also encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Niebla Castle&lt;/a&gt; on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Buey – Niebla Castle loop from San Juan del Puerto&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there scenic routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991332430/columbian-places-and-mazagon-coast-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Columbian Places and Mazagón Coast Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that combines historical landmarks with beautiful coastal views along the Atlantic near Mazagón. Additionally, the Estuary Promenade in Huelva, accessible from San Juan del Puerto, offers panoramic river views ideal for cycling.

Q: What natural features can be observed while cycling in the area?
A: Cyclists can experience the unique landscapes of the Tinto River, famous for its striking reddish hue, and its extensive marshes, including the protected Marismas del Odiel Natural Area, known for its flamingo population. The 'Vía Verde Los Molinos del Agua' also showcases the Huelva countryside with pines, cork oaks, and eucalyptus.

Q: Is San Juan del Puerto part of any long-distance cycling networks?
A: Yes, San Juan del Puerto is part of the EuroVelo 1 route, also known as the Atlantic Coast Route. This allows for maritime-themed journeys, connecting you to other significant towns like Moguer and Palos de la Frontera, and offering a broader exploration of the region.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in San Juan del Puerto?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the combination of natural beauty with historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that offer varied cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any greenways or dedicated cycling paths in the area?
A: Yes, the 'Vía Verde Los Molinos del Agua' starts in San Juan del Puerto, following part of an old railway line. This greenway provides a dedicated path through the Huelva countryside, offering a pleasant and safe cycling experience away from traffic.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through traditional villages or towns?
A: Many routes are designed to connect San Juan del Puerto with nearby towns and villages, allowing you to experience the local Andalusian charm. Routes often pass through places like Beas, Valverde del Camino, La Palma del Condado, and Niebla, offering opportunities to explore traditional architecture and local culture.
tips: One of the most beautiful in the province of Huelva, the Plaza de España in La Palma del Condado is the social center of this picturesque town. Surrounded by unique buildings such as the Casa de los Arcos, the Town Hall and the Church of San Juan Bautista, this beautiful place is chosen to have a good time at sunset on summer nights.

Its full name is the Church of Nuestra Señora de las Angustias, and it is located in the town of Villarrasa, a small town in Huelva with barely 2,300 inhabitants, on the street to which it has given the same name, Calle Angustias. This architectural work has participated in the filming of Western movies in which actors such as Mario Girotti, better known as Terence Hill, have acted.
It is an architectural construction from the 16th century that takes advantage of the foundations of previous buildings, the oldest part being the central nave. In later centuries the rest of the work was completed, incorporating Renaissance and Baroque elements, mainly on the façade, cornices, dome and dressing room.
Inside, it preserves a carving of Ntra. Sra. De las Angustias, a piety sculpted by Castillo Lastrucci in 1948, which replaces the one lost in 1936. Currently the church is also the headquarters of the Brotherhood of the Holy Christ of Goodness, which It constitutes one of the most emblematic images of the town of Villarrasa along with La Borriquita.
This is one of the few buildings in the town of Villarrasa that remained standing after the famous earthquake in Lisbon that hit practically the entire province of Huelva, leaving everything demolished in its path. In 2009 it underwent restoration work due to its poor condition, and that is why its external appearance has changed due to the color of the paint used and the brick reinforcements.
The historic almost 2000 years old Puente Romano crosses the Tinto river into Niebla
Alcázar o Castillo de los Guzmánes has some remnants of Roman construction, its 2000 years-old concrete mix survives better than contemporary concrete
Historic town with impressive ruins from Roman times, architecture from Arab occupations, and later.
Medieval southeastern city gate with mudejar elements
Its landscape is made up of agricultural land dedicated to the cultivation of cereals, vines and olive trees. As monuments of interest it has a Roman bridge and the Church of San Juan Bautista.
